name: Chitlins and Maw
recipeIngredient: 4 small green bell peppers, cored, seeded, and finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the pork maw thoroughly in several changes of cold water. Drain thoroughly.
2. Place the pork maw in a large pot with enough cold water to cover by 2 inches. Add the salt, red pepper, and half of the celery, onions, and green peppers. Heat to boiling, reduce to simmering, and cook, covered, until tender. This could take anywhere from 1 1/2 to 3 hours, depending on the maw.
3. Meanwhile, wash the chitlins carefully in several changes of cold water. Drain thoroughly.
4. Drain the cooked pork maw and reserve the cooking liquid. Place the chitlins in a large pot with enough of the pork maw cooking liquid to cover by 2 inches. Add the remaining celery, onions, and green peppers. Heat to boiling, reduce to simmering, and cook, covered, until tender, about 1 hour and 30 minutes.
5. When the pork maw is cool enough to handle, cut it into 1-inch pieces.
6. Stir in the pork maw pieces when the chitlins are tender and let simmer together a few minutes. Check the seasoning and serve hot.
